title: Gig Economy Worker: Tax Compliance and Financial Stability
goals: Resolve IRS underpayment notice and establish tax compliance
Build financial stability with variable income
constraints: No employer benefits of any kind
Personal auto insurance does not cover commercial use
key_risks: Uninsured during rideshare Period 1 (app on, no passenger)
No health insurance — catastrophic financial exposure
Zero retirement savings at age 30 — compounding time being lost

planning_issues.description: No quarterly estimated tax payments; IRS $8,200 underpayment with penalties accruing at 8% annually; SE tax 15.3% on net income
No health insurance; no rideshare TNC endorsement (personal policy voids during commercial use); no disability income
$6,200 savings = 2.3 months expenses; variable income workers need 6-month buffer minimum
Zero retirement savings at age 30; Solo 401k available for 1099 workers; also reduces SE tax burden

key_recommendations: Set up IRS installment agreement; establish 27% tax reserve account immediately
Set up quarterly estimated tax payments (January 15, April 15, June 15, September 15)
Enroll in ACA marketplace health insurance at next open enrollment (~$150-250/month after credits)
Open Solo 401k; contribute minimum 10% of net income; builds retirement and reduces SE tax
follow_up_questions: Does Jessica track mileage and vehicle expenses? (Standard mileage vs actual cost method)
Is Jessica enrolled in Uber Pro or Lyft rewards? (Gas/maintenance savings)
Has ACA enrollment ever been explored? Does she qualify for Medicaid?
Has an S-Corp election been considered to reduce SE tax at this income level?
outcome_summary: IRS underpayment paid off in 10 months. ACA Silver plan enrolled ($195/month after tax credits). Solo 401k opened with $4,500 contributed, reducing taxable income.
key_lesson: Gig workers must treat their finances as a small business — separate tax reserves, business-appropriate insurance, and Solo 401k contributions are foundational steps most 1099 workers skip.
